name: changelog-generation
description: From git history or conversation context, produce a structured changelog entry following Keep a Changelog conventions.

changes.description: Git log, commit messages, PR descriptions, or description of changes

steps.template:
Generate changelog for version {{version}} from:
{{changes}}

Format: {{format}}

Step 1 — Categorize changes:
- Added: New features
- Changed: Changes to existing functionality
- Deprecated: Features to be removed
- Removed: Features removed
- Fixed: Bug fixes
- Security: Vulnerability fixes

For each item:
- Write user-facing description (not commit message)
- Link to PR/issue if available
- Note breaking changes prominently

Step 2 — Format output:

For '{{format}}':
- keep-a-changelog: Markdown with ## [version] - date, grouped by category
- conventional: Type(scope): description per line
- github-release: Markdown with highlights, breaking changes callout, full list
- slack-summary: Brief emoji-tagged summary for team notification

Ensure:
- Most impactful changes listed first in each category
- Breaking changes have migration notes
- Language is clear to end users (not internal jargon)
